spec_and_test_updates.highlight: zkEVM spec v0.50 released: rebased on Glamsterdam and devnet 6; supports external transition tools (Geth) for benchmark fixture filling
SSZ max-length refinements in guest input will change hash tree root calculations — all implementers must review and adjust
Hive dashboard now tracks ethrex and Besu execution-witness compliance plus first guest program test runs in a single view
Bug fixed in Geth execution-witness generation (merged); benchmark fixtures refilled and looking correct

guest_api_standardization.highlight: 8 guest API standards accepted (ISA I/O, crypto accelerators, termination, memory, static library, linker script); ELF loading and accelerated memory operations open for review
ELF loading standard: recommendation is execute-and-read semantics for executable segments; execute-only (Zisk current) is discouraged as no toolchain guarantees instruction-only text sections
Precompiles in static libraries must not allocate on the guest heap; a separate isolated scratch region is required — other vendors should audit for this collision
Direction flagged: Ethereum guest programs should be built with stock unmodified toolchains to minimize trusted computing base

sync_protocol_and_recursive_proofs.highlight: Weak-subjectivity sync can require up to 113,000 slots (~26.9 GB of proof content); recursive execution proofs (SSZ Merkle tree or MMR) proposed to enable gossip-based chain-prefix authentication
Recursive proof approach would simplify consensus clients by eliminating complex request-response sync; proof-of-concept spec and implementation planned imminently

prover_infrastructure_and_benchmarking.highlight: EF cluster scalability: GPU proving scales near-linearly from 1 to full machine on large blocks; CPU-bound bottleneck observed between 2 and 4 GPUs
OOM kills still observed on mainnet EST 6M-gas blocks; root cause under investigation
New ZK-Boost release adapts to current compatible input format; proof-request and verification APIs updated to require chain config as public input

allocator_safety.highlight: Bump allocator (BAM) is fastest but causes OOM at current ~0.5 GB limit for high-gas blocks; increasing to 1.5 GB bypasses failures but leaves little headroom — sophisticated allocators (e.g., DLmalloc) recommended for safety
OpenVM 2.1 will support 4 GB of guest memory (chat: Yi Sun); most other zkVMs may not exceed 1 GB (Marcin)

evm_formal_specification.highlight: EVM SAIL spec (evm-sail) achieves 100% EAST test compliance; enables extraction to Lean, Isleris/Iris for binary compliance proofs and RISC-V guest program verification
Goal: SAIL spec reaches feature parity with Python execution specs, allowing core devs to contribute directly to a single formal specification

action_items.action: Review zkEVM spec v0.50 SSZ max-length changes and update hash tree root calculations accordingly
Add Nethermind (and other clients) to execution-witness Hive dashboard by adding a branch entry to the dashboard repo JSON
Audit precompile static libraries for guest-heap allocation collisions; add isolated scratch region if affected

decisions.decision: ELF loading standard adopts execute-and-read semantics for executable segments; execute-only interpretation is discouraged
Direction adopted: Ethereum guest programs must be built with unmodified stock toolchains to limit trusted computing base
Bump allocator not recommended for production guest programs due to OOM liveness risk at higher gas limits; sophisticated allocators preferred

targets.target: Recursive proof chain-prefix PoC spec and implementation — Francesco, coming days
Next zkEVM release to include both test fixtures and Geth-filled benchmark fixtures
